with 1t, and unless good faith is


shown by the committee, the mayor
and the council in allowing the com.


mittee to be increased to five, and
allowing it to work in secret, its


report will gain no value from


cooperation of the only people in
town who have been doing any
worth while investigation.
And other means of securing the
truth, and of informing the public,


will be adopted by the three organ,


iZations.


The reply to the. committee. fol-
lows:


SPOKANE, March 14-Members
of the Committee on Investigation
of Conditions at the City Hall-Gen-
tlemen The undersigned having
received letter of notification that
your committee will meet Monday
March IA. at 7:30 p. M, for the pur-
pose of hearing charges and evil
dence concerning conditions at the
city Jail, and extending an invita-
ton to us to appear at such meet
ing. wish to say in reply to such
invitation that while we have not
made any charges against the po-
lice department, yet can present
witnesses and statements relative
to past conditions in the city jail.
This evidence, however, can only
be presented before your commit
tee in secret session and under such
conditions as will give absolute pro-
lection to every witness whom we
may present. $3.50. ...- ;..--


If the sessions at which such evil
dence and witnesses are to be pre-
scented are held openly and with
knowledge of the police department
as to time and place of such meet
ings, the witnesses will not appear
for fear of intimidation and perse-
cution uDon the part of said police
department. .........


Now that an investigation is de-
sired by the city council. we will
gladly cooperate in your work by
presenting the evidence and wit
nesses on the subject of your inves-
tigation. but only under the condi-
tons stipulated above Respect
fully yours. Laura NJ. House, May A.
Hutton. Karl Riedelsberger, Mrs.
Philip P. Stafford.
